The risk is to then interpret that as that they will be looking for more intrinsic value and again to discriminate between one kind of value and the other but they don't. They will be looking for more value for their buck but value of all kinds and the IPU kind not just the intrinsic kind. So who do you for example who do you think is more endangered now and coming out the back end of this six flags are Disney? Sure six flags of course because they have they they're working with 80% of their value intrinsic only 20% IPU their portfolio their toolbox of ways that they can create value is so limited and so restricted they're more endangered right. So the big thing out the back of this is that our consumers are going to want more value for their buck but we have to be very careful not to restrict in our own mind the way we deliver the value create the value or one kind of value over another they just want more value in order to spend their money. We have at the Cleveland Playhouse right now we have the Broadway production of our buried poppins and and you're just tall enough to go they have a height limit and you get in for half price. That's value yeah and and I talked to them and they said look we making a big deal out of you we can't just pitch a play anymore it's a whole we got to make sure they understand that that there's special effects you know there's people flying through the air and there's all that going on and that all of the actors come out afterwards and stay as long as it takes to meet with everybody and take pictures and sign stuff and there's a pre-play party and there's because we can't just sell play right now we got to sell something bigger better more interesting more exciting and yet really the intrinsic values to play all the other stuff is a different kind of value but same old same old and the same for their don't donors so this connects to this idea Buffett who basically buys businesses as you know so his quote is good businesses are in some way reasonably sheltered from competition they have a franchise of some sort right so they have some point of differentiation that they are built and are building on that they have a means of protecting right so my leap about all that is is that we have to be focused on what's sustainable and I'm extremely suspicious about investing in what is not sustainable so the equivalent of hey nobody's got coffee makers in the room let's put coffee makers in the room not really exciting to me as an idea for the idea board because anybody can get a coffee maker and there's an empty outlet in every room so how long are we going to be able to sustain that right I'm more interested in what can we try and protect and sustain when we create value and for the most part it all ultimately at the high-income levels all comes back to you so what do people who earn seven figures actually get paid for well they tend to have mastered one or more high-level competencies they do tend to be really really really really really really really good highly skilled if not talented at something and we're talking about mastery in the context of productivity as well as well maybe as important they get paid for being extremely self-confident and superior in a way they present themselves
